attributes.description: Alerts when local disk read throughput exceeds a threshold over the lookback window. Important: the DiskReadBytes metric only measures temporary local disks (instance store) that are physically attached to the host. It does NOT measure EBS volumes, which is how most EC2 instances store their data. If your instances use EBS (the common case), this alert will stay silent — track EBS disk activity using the EBS metrics (AWS/EBS namespace) instead.
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] High disk read throughput

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND attributes.MetricName == "DiskReadBytes"
    AND attributes.stat == "Sum"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(InstanceId, " (", resource.attributes.cloud.region, ")")
| STATS total_read_bytes = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskReadBytes`) BY ec2_instance
// Bytes threshold: tune per instance size and workload — Sum stat is bytes per CloudWatch period
| WHERE total_read_bytes > 536870912
| SORT total_read_bytes DESC
| LIMIT 100
